title: Object.create
body:
## Definition

**`Object.create(proto)`** creates a new object with the specified object as its `[[Prototype]]` — or `null` for a dictionary with no inherited properties.

## Why it exists

Explicit prototype wiring without constructors; pure map objects without `Object.prototype` pollution.

## How it works

```js
const dict = Object.create(null);
dict.count = 1; // no inherited toString/hasOwnProperty

const child = Object.create(parent);
```

Alternative to `new Constructor()` when you only need prototype linkage.

## Common confusion

`Object.create({})` still inherits from `Object.prototype` via the empty object proto. Use `null` for true dictionaries.

## In practice

Safe maps, prototype delegation patterns, and polyfilling `Object.create` in older environments.

## Definition

**`Object.create(proto)`** creates a [new](concept:new-operator) object with the specified object as its [[[Prototype]]](concept:prototype-chain) — or [null](concept:null-value) for a dictionary with no inherited properties.

## Why it exists

Explicit [prototype](concept:prototype-chain) wiring without constructors; pure [map objects](concept:map-data-structure) without [Object.prototype](concept:prototype-chain) pollution.

## How it works

```js
const dict = Object.create(null);
dict.count = 1; // no inherited toString/hasOwnProperty

const child = Object.create(parent);
```

Alternative to `new Constructor()` when you only need [prototype](concept:prototype-chain) linkage.

## Common confusion

`Object.create({})` still inherits from [Object.prototype](concept:prototype-chain) via the empty object proto. Use [null](concept:null-value) for true dictionaries.

## In practice

Safe maps, [prototype](concept:prototype-chain) delegation patterns, and polyfilling [Object.create](concept:object-create) in older environments.
